title: Heaps
description: Binary heaps: the heap property, array representation, insertion and extraction by sifting, building a heap in linear time, heapsort, and the priority queue ADT.
objectives: State the heap property for a min-heap and a max-heap, and explain why a heap is stored as a complete binary tree
Derive and use the array-index formulas for a node's parent and children
Trace insertion into a heap, including the sift-up (bubble-up) step, and state its time complexity
Trace extraction of the root from a heap, including the sift-down (bubble-down) step, and state its time complexity
Explain how build-heap (heapify) turns an arbitrary array into a heap in O(n), and how heapsort uses a heap to sort in place
Describe the priority queue ADT and identify heaps as its standard efficient implementation, with examples of its use

lessons.title: The heap property and array representation
